claims.summary: Small RCTs suggest modest immune parameter improvements (NK cell activity, cytokine balance). Effects inconsistent across studies.

clinical_protocol.standard: RCTs used standardized extracts (1-3g daily of fruiting body extract, standardized to 10-30% polysaccharides and 1-5% triterpenes). Hot-water extracts for polysaccharides, alcohol extracts for triterpenes.
clinical_protocol.traditional_equivalence: Traditional TCM use involves 6-12g dried fruiting body decocted (boiled) for hours, often in multi-herb formulas. Modern mushroom powder capsules use whole ground fruiting body at similar doses but without the long extraction time.

provenance.commercial_drift_note: Modern clinical trials use standardized extracts (10-30% polysaccharides, 1-5% triterpenes) from hot-water or dual extraction. Traditional TCM uses long decoction of whole fruiting body (6-12g). Commercial mushroom powder capsules often use unextracted whole fruiting body powder, which has much lower bioavailability of active compounds compared to studied extracts.

claims.summary: Mixed results in cancer-related fatigue. Some RCTs show benefit, others no effect vs placebo.
No modern clinical trials. Classical TCM use as 'spirit tonic' for calming, meditation support, and longevity. First mentioned in Shennong Bencao Jing (2nd century CE).
